name: European-Commission-Prompt-Implementation
description: Horizon Europe Implementation section (Section 3: Work Plan, Resources, Consortium Capacity)

variants.name: default
variants.text:
You are writing SECTION 3: QUALITY AND EFFICIENCY OF THE IMPLEMENTATION of a Horizon Europe grant proposal (Part B - Technical Description). INPUTS: - {{content}}: The researcher's actual work, publications, and technical expertise. This is your PRIMARY source for understanding what activities need to be done, what expertise the team brings, and what infrastructure is available. - {{grant_info}}: The specific Horizon Europe call and topic description, including Topic ID, Type of Action, expected duration, budget range, consortium requirements, and any specific requirements. YOUR TASK: Generate ONLY Section 3: Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation. Do NOT generate Section 1 (Excellence), Section 2 (Impact), or any other proposal component. Those are handled separately. EVALUATION CRITERION: Quality and effectiveness of the work plan, assessment of risks, and appropriateness of the effort assigned to work packages and resources overall. Capacity and role of each participant, and extent to which the consortium brings together the necessary expertise. SECTION STRUCTURE: 3.1 WORK PLAN AND RESOURCES (approximately 12 pages) Overall Work Plan Structure: - Describe the overall logic and organization of work packages based on the activities required to deliver the objectives from {{content}} - Explain how the work packages build on each other and relate to the research described in {{content}} - Propose 5-8 work packages proportionate to the project scale indicated in {{grant_info}} Standard Work Package Structure (adapt based on {{content}} and {{grant_info}}): - WP1: Project Management — coordination, quality assurance, financial management, risk management - WP2-WPX: Research and Innovation WPs — organized around the technical objectives derived from {{content}}, each addressing specific research activities described in {{content}} - WP(X+1): Dissemination, Exploitation, and Communication — implementation of the D&E&C plan - WP(X+2): Ethics Requirements — only if {{content}} or {{grant_info}} indicates ethics-sensitive activities For Each Work Package, describe: - Objectives: what this WP achieves in the context of {{content}} - Tasks: break down into specific activities grounded in the methods and work described in {{content}} * For each task: describe activities, quantify where possible (number of experiments, prototypes, datasets, publications), specify who does the work and why their expertise from {{content}} qualifies them - Deliverables: concrete outputs from each WP - Milestones: key decision points or verification events - Role of each participant: what specific expertise from {{content}} each partner contributes Gantt Chart (describe as text table): - Show timing of all work packages across the project duration from {{grant_info}} - Show start/end months, overlaps, and dependencies - Mark key milestones and deliverables Required Tables (use plain text format): Table 3.1a — List of Work Packages: WP No | WP Title | Lead Participant | Person-Months | Start Month | End Month (populate based on activities in {{content}} and duration from {{grant_info}}) Table 3.1b — Work Package Descriptions: (one per WP, with objectives, task descriptions, deliverables, milestones, partner roles) Table 3.1c — List of Deliverables: Number | Deliverable Name | Short Description | WP No | Lead | Type | Dissemination Level | Delivery Month - Include mandatory deliverables: Data Management Plan (month 6), Dissemination and Exploitation Plan (month 6) - Type codes: R (report), DEM (demonstrator/prototype), DEC (website/media), DATA (datasets), DMP, ETHICS, OTHER - Dissemination codes: PU (public), SEN (sensitive) Table 3.1d — List of Milestones: Milestone No | Name | Related WPs | Due Month | Means of Verification - Milestones should correspond to key achievements that enable the next phase of work described in {{content}} Table 3.1e — Critical Risks: Risk Description (Likelihood: L/M/H, Severity: L/M/H) | WPs Involved | Mitigation Measures - Identify risks specific to the technical approach described in {{content}} - Types: technical (methods in {{content}} not performing as expected), scientific (hypotheses not validated), resource, partnership, data, ethical/legal - For each risk: preventive actions, contingency plans, monitoring approach Table 3.1f — Summary of Staff Effort: Rows: each participant | Columns: WP1, WP2, ... WPX, Total Person-Months - Effort must be realistic for the activities described in {{content}} - Bold the person-month figure for the WP leader Table 3.1g — Subcontracting (if applicable): Participant | Cost (EUR) | Tasks and Justification - Only for specialized tasks that cannot be performed by consortium partners - Core research activities from {{content}} should NOT be subcontracted Table 3.1h — Major Equipment (if applicable): Participant | Cost (EUR) | Justification - Only required if equipment costs exceed 15% of personnel costs for a participant - Justify based on the specific experimental or technical needs described in {{content}} 3.2 CAPACITY OF PARTICIPANTS AND CONSORTIUM AS A WHOLE (approximately 3 pages) IMPORTANT: Do NOT repeat individual participant descriptions from Part A. Focus on consortium composition, complementarity, and collective capacity. Consortium Composition and Rationale: - Describe how the consortium composition matches the expertise required to execute the work described in {{content}} - Show how the consortium covers all required expertise areas: technical/scientific (as described in {{content}}), SSH (if required by {{grant_info}}), dissemination and exploitation capabilities - Demonstrate European added value: why does this specific transnational partnership strengthen the work? - Show geographical distribution and how it enhances the project (access to different markets, regulatory environments, user groups) - If {{grant_info}} requires specific consortium composition (e.g., minimum SME involvement, specific country requirements), confirm compliance Individual Partner Roles: For each partner: - Specific role in the project and why this partner is necessary - Key expertise relevant to the activities in {{content}} — what unique capability do they bring? - Which WPs they lead and contribute to - Access to critical infrastructure needed for the work described in {{content}} (laboratories, testing facilities, computing resources, clinical sites, user communities) Complementarity and Synergies: - Explain how partners' expertise fits together to deliver the work described in {{content}} - Describe collaboration mechanisms: joint tasks, secondments, workshops, shared data - If partners have previously collaborated, reference this as evidence of working relationship Industrial/Commercial Involvement (particularly for IA): - If {{grant_info}} indicates an Innovation Action, describe how industry partners ensure exploitation of results from {{content}} - Highlight SME participation and their role in innovation and exploitation Non-EU Partners (if applicable): - If any partner is from outside EU/Associated Countries, justify their participation - Explain what unique expertise, infrastructure, or access they provide that is not available within the EU - Demonstrate that project objectives from {{content}} cannot be achieved without them WRITING GUIDELINES: - Ground every work package and task in the actual activities required to deliver the research described in {{content}} - Be specific about who does what — vague role descriptions will weaken the evaluation - Ensure effort (person-months) is realistic for the activities described - Risk table should reflect genuine risks to the technical approach in {{content}}, not generic project risks - Do NOT include URLs to external websites - Do NOT fabricate partner capabilities not supported by {{content}} - Define all acronyms on first use - Use plain text for all tables (must be encodable in EU grant management IT systems) ALIGNMENT: - Read {{grant_info}} for expected project duration, budget range, and consortium requirements — structure work packages accordingly - If {{grant_info}} specifies a Type of Action (RIA vs IA), calibrate the balance between research WPs and innovation/demonstration WPs - If {{grant_info}} requires ethics procedures, include a dedicated Ethics WP OUTPUT FORMAT: Generate well-structured HTML: - Use

for the section title Section 3: Quality and Efficiency of the Implementation - Use

for subsections (3.1, 3.2) - Use

for work package titles and table headings - Use

for paragraph content - Use //
/ for all tables - Use
    /
  • for lists - Use for emphasis sparingly - Target approximately 15 pages (7,000-9,000 words)
